name: chokepoint-byosi
bodyMarkdown:
# BYOSI Scripting Interpreters — Detection Chokepoint

**Priority:** HIGH | **Tactic:** Defense Evasion, Execution | **Difficulty:** HIGH | **Prevalence:** EMERGING
**Techniques:** T1059.006, T1059.007, T1059, T1027.010, T1218

## Overview
Attackers bring vendor-signed interpreters (Python, PHP, Node.js, etc.) to bypass EDR binary analysis. Chokepoint: non-default interpreter binary on disk → process launch → attacker script execution.

## Chokepoint Stages

### 1. Interpreter Deployment
Non-default interpreter introduced to system.
Observable: EID 11 (vendor-signed interpreter in user-writable directory), EID 7 (interpreter DLLs in-memory).

### 2. Interpreter Execution
Non-default interpreter launched.
Observable: EID 1 (python.exe/node.exe from non-standard path).

### 3. Malicious Script Action
Script performs observable action.
Observable: EID 3 (outbound), EID 1 (child processes), EID 11/12/13 (file/registry writes).

## Variations (8 tracked)
BYOSI PHP (oldkingcone), PolyDrop (13 languages), IronNetInjector (Turla APT), Node.js Malware, Lu0Bot, PYC Evasion, BYOI .NET (IronPython/Boolang), AutoHotKey Abuse.

## Detection Logic
- **Research:** Non-default interpreters from user-writable paths
- **Hunt:** Interpreter from unusual path + script args or inline exec flags
- **Analyst:** Interpreter dropped to temp → executed by suspicious parent → outbound C2
